text.title: Cold Outreach Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Cold Outreach. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: ≤200 prospects per cycle. Larger = lower personalisation = lower reply rate.
rule.rationale: Above 200, personalisation collapses + spam-trap risk rises.

rule.statement: Every message has at least one verifiable personal data point in the first line.
Sequence has 4-5 steps spaced 2-3 days apart. Shorter = under-touched; longer = annoying.
rule.rationale: Optimal reply count lands between step 3 and 5.

rule.statement: Sequence stops on reply OR bounce immediately. Continuing past reply = lawsuit risk + reputation hit.
rule.rationale: Continuing past reply is a complaint trigger.

rule.statement: Daily send cap follows warmup curve (start 20/day, ramp ≤10% per day).
rule.rationale: Spikes from a warmed domain still trip spam filters.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-growth-cold-outreach.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
